113135.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief believes that the male/female ratio should not be an end in itself. What is most important is that the mayor and aldermen can perform their duties well; it is about knowledge and skills.

113137.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief is pleased that the municipality currently recognizes the right to challenge. We consider this a great initiative and would like to continue it. We want residents to contribute to society. More attention should be given to this right.

113139.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief believes farmers should be allowed small ancillary activities on their farms, as long as this fits the environment and does not hinder residents and businesses. Farmers must be given the opportunity to undertake business activities.

113143.explanation: The use of labor migrants for seasonal work occurs in Duiven but on a small scale. We do not find it realistic to ask farmers to provide this housing. There is already a location in Duiven for housing labor migrants, and availability remains there.

113147.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief is in favor of fireworks shows in Duiven, Groessen, and Loo. The municipality should not organize these itself but should enable initiatives. Also, it should be possible for associations to be granted an exception to the ban as currently proposed by the national government.

113150.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief values an inclusive society where everyone can participate. Entrepreneurs should be encouraged to contribute, but the responsibility remains with the entrepreneur.

113155.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief believes music education should be provided in primary schools. This already happens with the help of music associations and volunteers. We would like to continue this. Children should also keep coming into contact with culture.

113158.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief finds street safety important; more BOA officers are needed here. With more BOAs, they can better perform youth work, helping to combat nuisance from loitering youth and connecting young people to society.

113159.explanation: We believe that additional camera surveillance is not an end in itself, but if there are locations where it is necessary, this consideration can be made per location.

113160.explanation: We are not aware of any need here. Locations already exist in the region. If there is need and necessity, we are open to it.

113165.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief does not want large-scale construction and uncontrolled growth in rural areas. We want to allow it only in a limited way and on their own property where possible.

113166.explanation: We believe people on social welfare should be employed as soon as possible, and the municipality should facilitate this. Everyone has the right to participate in society. Everyone should be able to exercise this right.

113168.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief wants to prevent a harsh poverty trap. By not setting a hard limit but using different income thresholds for different regulations, the poverty trap is limited.

113170.explanation: Currently, there are already sufficient places. We prefer to realize charging points as much as possible in the private driveway. However, if the need and space in the neighborhood are sufficient, a public charging point can be installed.

113171.explanation: We believe that where possible, through roads should have a 50 km/h speed limit to promote calm and traffic flow. Residential areas and roads without a connecting function should have a 30 km/h speed limit.

113172.explanation: The shortage and high costs in healthcare make this unfeasible. Therefore, we do not want extra restrictions; we do not have that luxury. We do hope that existing care providers can better accommodate faith, culture, or conviction.

113173.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief believes the costs and quality of care and youth aid must be balanced. Cost savings should not come at the expense of the quality of youth aid; young people need it. Quality ensures savings in the long term.

113174.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief wants to limit tax increases for residents and businesses as much as possible. Therefore, we would also like to see the property tax lowered, but only on the condition that the municipality's level of service does not decline.

113176.explanation: In the municipality of Duiven, we already have a high tourist tax. We do not want to increase it further. We should actually promote tourism.

113177.explanation: Business parks are important for the economy, employment, and municipal revenue. They should be expanded based on demand. We must look at the limit, where to stop and not go further. This way, we also preserve green zones and the landscape.

113181.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief prefers other methods of energy generation and wants to preserve our agricultural land. We believe the municipality should stimulate this; immediate prohibition is not a priority.

113182.explanation: The Ogtent already receives subsidies from the municipality. We consider this subsidy sufficient; the Ogtent also has commercial functions that generate income.

113183.explanation: Residents can receive money for swimming lessons through minimum income schemes. We find this arrangement sufficient, and therefore we see no need to introduce school swimming.

113184.explanation: We find it important to learn more about the Liemers culture. The Liemers dialect is an important part of it. That is why we want primary schools to pay attention to it, so the Liemers dialect does not get lost.

113185.explanation: The percentage depends on what is needed. Currently, it is 30%, which we do not want to increase further. Also, building mid-range and expensive owner-occupied homes promotes housing turnover. Additionally, we find a good mix important.

113187.explanation: Lokaal Alternatief is glad that the municipality is actively working to make this possible. This helps students, young people, and other home seekers find housing more quickly.

113188.explanation: Expanding parking spaces is not an end in itself for us. The needs should be assessed per neighborhood. Also, we can look at how to ensure fewer cars in the neighborhood.

113190.explanation: Duiven is stronger in one Liemers municipality. Together we keep facilities affordable and nearby, strengthen our regional position, and prevent the merger of Westervoort and Arnhem. Village identity remains preserved through local involvement and community life. For a future-oriented Duiven.

113191.explanation: Local Alternative wants to keep the green space between Groessen and Duiven open. We do not want any new housing development in this area besides the development of Biezenkampen. The housing shortage is so severe that Biezenkampen must be realized. This way, starters can continue to live in our municipality.

113192.explanation: Local Alternative wants to first discuss with the province the consequences for Broekstraat and Oostsingel. We want residents of Groessen and De Nieuweling to continue to access the center of Duiven via the Rijksweg. The Holland road is not an alternative for the through road.

113135.explanation: What is decisive is that society as a whole is represented, not the numerical ratio between men and women. Therefore, an equal distribution in the executive board is not necessary.

113137.explanation: We believe in the power of volunteer work and encourage and support initiatives by residents themselves.

113139.explanation: Entrepreneurship is the foundation of our prosperity. Therefore, we believe there must be room for small-scale campsites and shops at farms.

113143.explanation: We feel it is a task for the sector itself and should not be solved by the municipality. However, it is important to set requirements that housing must meet.

113147.explanation: We believe that if a definitive fireworks ban applies, the municipality should offer associations the possibility to set off fireworks in a controlled manner, with active involvement of young people.

113150.explanation: Short term extra effort is needed to improve accessibility for people with disabilities. Financial support for entrepreneurs is not part of this, but actively thinking along about solutions is.

113155.explanation: Music lessons are important to stimulate the creativity of children. This should therefore be encouraged by the municipality.

113158.explanation: We advocate expanding (visible!) enforcement in the municipality of Duiven to promote safety.

113159.explanation: We support the use of camera surveillance because it increases safety in our society.

113160.explanation: We believe women in vulnerable situations must always have access to a safe and reliable sanctuary.

113165.explanation: To address the housing shortage, more and faster new construction is necessary. Village boundaries should not be an obstacle so that sufficient housing can be realized in suitable locations on time.

113166.explanation: We believe participation is better promoted by encouraging socially meaningful work than by imposing obligations.

113168.explanation: The current income threshold does not sufficiently align with the current cost of living. A higher threshold ensures that municipal assistance better fits the financial reality of residents.

113170.explanation: We believe that when expanding charging points for electric cars, existing parking spaces must be handled carefully so this does not come at the expense of parking spaces for non-electric cars.

113171.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km per hour contributes to a safe and clear traffic situation in the municipality of Duiven and therefore has our support.

113172.explanation: We believe that people who need care already lose enough self-reliance; they should not be expected to give up even more.

113173.explanation: Absolutely not. For us, the quality of help is paramount; additionally, the speed of providing help can also play a role.

113174.explanation: We agree with a regular inflation adjustment. Additional increases solely intended to balance municipal finances we consider unnecessary.

113176.explanation: Tourist tax has already been raised; moreover, the municipality of Duiven is not a tourist destination. With the recent VAT increase on hotel stays, we believe tourists should not be endlessly burdened with extra taxes.

113177.explanation: Since many buildings are still vacant, we consider expansion unnecessary at this time and advocate first making smarter and more efficient use of existing buildings before adding new construction.

113181.explanation: We find green energy important. Many commercial buildings and houses have unused roofs where solar panels can be placed without taking away (agricultural) land.

113182.explanation: The Ogtent contributes to the culture in the municipality and must retain its right to exist. The municipality can play a more active role in exploring and supporting potential opportunities. More funding is not needed.

113183.explanation: We fully support mandatory school swimming lessons so that everyone can swim safely in our water-rich environment.

113184.explanation: We believe schools themselves, within the Ministry of Education’s guidelines, should determine how they use their available teaching hours. It is not necessary for the municipality to actively stimulate this.

113185.explanation: We believe there must be a good balance between social rental and middle-rent housing. An increase in the share of social rental housing is negotiable, but a minimum of 40% is not acceptable to us.

113187.explanation: We focus on simpler rental of living space to reduce the shortage in the short term, with clear rules to prevent misuse.

113188.explanation: We note that car usage and ownership are high and advocate for a neighborhood-oriented inventory of the need for extra parking spaces.

113190.explanation: DOED sees no benefits in a merger. Through cooperation, we achieve the same goals, save money for residents and associations. Furthermore, a merger increases the distance between residents and the municipality, which we find undesirable.

113191.explanation: We support housing development on the Biezenkampen, provided this is done in close consultation with neighbors (participation) and new buildings are no higher than three stories.

113192.explanation: We focus on less traffic in residential areas and better municipal access. A carefully planned bypass, connected to the N810, could possibly be a solution.

113135.explanation: The municipal government should be a true reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

113137.explanation: If you give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, beautiful things arise. The municipality should not hinder this but rather encourage it.

113139.explanation: Agriculture must become much more sustainable, but we also want farmers to earn money with their farms. A store selling good products from local land or a nice campsite can help with this. Therefore, we want to enable these options.

113143.explanation: Those who bring people to our municipality to work must ensure these workers have decent housing without burdening our residents. There is no place for exploitation and nuisance in our municipality.

113147.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all enjoy together, with one large fireworks show organized by the municipality. This way, we keep it safe and can all enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

113150.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide support.

113155.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

113158.explanation: Thanks to a proposal we previously submitted to the municipal council, extra enforcement officers (BOA’s) have been added, and that works well. They are visible in the neighborhood, can intervene quickly in case of nuisance, and are approachable for young people. More BOA’s help keep our neighborhoods safe and pleasant.

113159.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the only solution. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. That is why we prefer more enforcers on the street who can also immediately intervene when something goes wrong.

113160.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. A shelter for domestic violence victims is indispensable for our municipality.

113165.explanation: We want to preserve and protect the greenery around our living areas as best as possible. But to tackle the housing shortage, we must also use agricultural land for housing. This is also an opportunity to create pleasant, green neighborhoods for our residents.

113166.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

113168.explanation: More and more people are financially struggling, even those just above the current income threshold. By raising that threshold, we can help more residents on time and prevent small problems from becoming big ones. This way, we keep Duiven social and accessible for everyone.

113170.explanation: We install charging poles where there is demand. Not for the sake of installing them, but because more and more people are driving electric. As demand grows in a neighborhood, the number of charging points increases accordingly. This keeps it fair, practical, and appropriate to residents' needs.

113171.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

113172.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don’t solve this by cutting more and always choosing the cheapest options. We must especially ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

113173.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don’t solve this by cutting more and always choosing the cheapest options. We must especially ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

113174.explanation: Companies like Ikea, Fedex, and Intratuin receive an 80% tax discount from the municipality of Duiven compared to neighboring municipalities, while municipal taxes for residents are above average. This could be distributed much more fairly while we continue to defend our facilities.

113176.explanation: We invest in the landscape of our municipality and make it possible to develop more tourist activities. This also makes it reasonable to ask tourists for a contribution towards this.

113177.explanation: We are expanding business parks, partly to relocate companies from areas where space becomes available for housing development. This way, Duiven contributes to regional agreements. We choose innovative companies and starting entrepreneurs, not large logistics centers or fast-food zones.

113181.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

113182.explanation: We want better collaboration and better utilization of cultural facilities. The OGtent should be more strongly connected with residents and partners like Liemers Kunstwerk. If it becomes clear that extra resources are needed to strengthen the cultural offerings, we will make that possible.

113183.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and every child should learn to swim. Including children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not fairly arranged. The best solution: school swimming for every child, with support from the municipality.

113184.explanation: It is valuable if children learn about the history of the Liemers at school. That strengthens their bond with the region and helps retain young talent here. We already have beautiful places that tell this story, such as Fort Geldersoord, the dike stories, and the Liemers Museum. Let's make use of them.

113185.explanation: Owner-occupied homes have become unattainable for many people. Social rental housing is accessible for a broad group, but enough must be built. Therefore, the share of social rental housing in new construction must increase significantly, so everyone retains a fair chance to live here.

113187.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage, and many people in our municipality live more spaciously than necessary. That is why we want to make it easier to rent out individual rooms, provided they are well-maintained and rented for a fair price.

113188.explanation: More parking spaces do not relieve the pressure. In Duiven, car usage is above average because bike paths and walking routes are unclear or unattractive. We prefer to invest in better public transport, safe cycling routes, and affordable housing to limit the number of cars.

113190.explanation: Duiven does not need to remain independent. The Liemers region faces major challenges that we can better address together. By merging, we strengthen healthcare, housing, sustainability, and amenities. This creates one strong municipality that stays close to residents and can do more for the region.

113191.explanation: The meadows between Duiven and Groessen do not have to remain empty. Here the green, car-free neighborhood Biezenkampen can be developed with about 300 homes. This provides space for many residents and ensures flow. However, the landscape must grow along with parks and good facilities.

113192.explanation: Widening the Plaksestraat is necessary to start creating a ring road that keeps car traffic out of the center and neighborhoods. Residents fear more traffic, but this helps make Duiven more car-free. Outside the village, the car; inside the village, space for cyclists.

113135.explanation: The ambition for this is certainly present but depends on the portfolios and backgrounds involved.

113137.explanation: This is indeed a challenge, but with sufficient financial support, it certainly deserves a chance.

113139.explanation: Only as long as it is connected to the company and its immediate surroundings and is considered a side activity.

113143.explanation: Agreed that they should provide housing. That is only possible as a group at a larger-scale location.

113147.explanation: The municipality must provide space for groups of residents who want to organize something like this and assist them accordingly.

113150.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for that.

113155.explanation: Although music is important, it is up to schools to decide how they spend their resources.

113158.explanation: At this time, the number of enforcement officers (boa's) is sufficient, and this is periodically evaluated.

113159.explanation: At this moment, there is no obligation. That may change in the future, so we do not rule it out.

113160.explanation: Certainly if there is a necessity for it!

113165.explanation: Housing development in rural areas is acceptable, but only adjacent to villages, if there is space and if it is acceptable from a planning perspective.

113166.explanation: It may certainly be requested with appropriate guidance.

113168.explanation: It is questionable whether this will solve the problem. Assistance should be more focused on support.

113170.explanation: Unless there is a demonstrable shortage, so not by definition. Policy has already been developed for this.

113171.explanation: Where these roads must also be designed according to the requirements for 30 km/h zones. Simply placing a '30' sign is not sufficient.

113173.explanation: It is about the quality that must be delivered. Price is certainly important but not the most important condition.

113174.explanation: Apart from inflation adjustment, we will continue to strive for that.

113176.explanation: The rate may certainly be increased with inflation adjustment.

113177.explanation: Where we have possibilities, it is certainly worthwhile. But not at the expense of nature reserves.

113181.explanation: Where they are currently allowed (within existing plans), they may still be built. We do not want to use new agricultural land for this.

113182.explanation: The subsidy they receive must be sufficient.

113183.explanation: Subsidies are already given under conditions for children's swimming lessons. School swimming is too costly for a municipality or a school.

113184.explanation: This relates to preserving identity and fosters connection.

113187.explanation: Where this is possible, it is certainly worth the effort.

113188.explanation: If there is a shortage, we should look with the neighborhood to see if and how this can be resolved.

113190.explanation: A municipal merger has advantages and disadvantages, just like remaining independent. We must carefully weigh these together. CDA Duiven-Groessen-Loo is not fundamentally opposed to a municipal merger.

113191.explanation: Empty in the sense that it maintains the farming activity and preservation of the natural landscape.

113192.explanation: As far as we are concerned, not the Plaksestraat. However, a new connection could be made between Heiliglandsestraat and Oostsingel. The provincial coupled sale (closing Broekstraat to motorized traffic, which we do not support) is still a bottleneck.

113135.explanation: We prioritize qualities, not gender balance.

113137.explanation: It is good that residents can take responsibility for certain topics.

113139.explanation: To a certain extent, that should be possible.

113143.explanation: Alone or together with other agricultural and horticultural businesses.

113147.explanation: Not the municipality, but perhaps an organization can take care of this. The municipality should facilitate, not produce.

113150.explanation: The municipality facilitates this for its own buildings, not for third-party buildings.

113160.explanation: There is currently no reason for that, the regional shelter meets our expectations.

113165.explanation: In certain cases, this is conceivable. We must handle that carefully.

113166.explanation: Everyone should contribute to society. Even people with benefits can mean something to society.

113168.explanation: The higher the limit, the harder it becomes to become independent.

113170.explanation: Eventually, every second parking spot must have a charging point. However, we must arrange it so that these spots are not exclusively for charging.

113171.explanation: In certain cases, this is not feasible or wise. Emergency services are disadvantaged by reducing speeds.

113172.explanation: People should be able to choose for themselves; the municipality does not need to contribute extra for this. So a more expensive choice is up to the person and they should bear the extra costs.

113173.explanation: The VVD favors capped prices for the assistance provided.

113174.explanation: We must always be critical of every tax.

113176.explanation: We must always be critical of every tax.

113177.explanation: We still have opportunities; it would be a shame not to take advantage of them.

113181.explanation: This is not up to the municipality but to higher authorities to decide.

113182.explanation: The subsidy for the OGtent is balanced as far as we are concerned.

113183.explanation: This is not a municipal task.

113184.explanation: The VVD finds it more important that our youth masters Dutch correctly.

113187.explanation: In certain cases, that must be possible.

113188.explanation: Parking is well arranged in Duiven.

113190.explanation: In the future, we cannot remain independent. Therefore, we must make the right choices now before it is too late and we have no options left.

113191.explanation: De Biezenkampen is for us an expansion location for housing, fitting within the environment. Furthermore, we want to maintain a green buffer between the different villages.

113192.explanation: There must be a connection between Heiliglandsestraat and Oostsingel.

113135.explanation: Knowledge is more important than gender.

113137.explanation: To ensure continuity, this is not desirable.

113143.explanation: They should not depend on an employer for housing.

113172.explanation: Care is care and should not depend on limiting factors.

113135.explanation: We look at who can do the work best. Not at gender, but at quality. At the same time, we want a council as diverse as possible so that everyone sees themselves reflected in the administration.

113137.explanation: Yes, we think that is a good idea. If residents have a good plan, they should be able to take over management of a sports hall or park. This gives people more space and responsibility. That strengthens involvement in the neighborhood.

113139.explanation: Not always. We look at quality and the plan. Sometimes a shop or campsite fits well, sometimes not. We want to give farmers space, but also care for nature, livability, and a good balance.

113143.explanation: Employers who arrange housing themselves ensure clear agreements and good living conditions. This way, we prevent pressure on the housing market and keep homes available for permanent residents. This is fair and good for everyone.

113147.explanation: With a fireworks show, we celebrate the new year together. It is festive, safe, and causes less damage. This way, New Year's remains cozy and sustainable. We choose a solution that preserves tradition while taking account of people and nature.

113150.explanation: People with disabilities deserve equal opportunities. Therefore, we support entrepreneurs in making buildings accessible. With financial aid, we create together a municipality where everyone is welcome. This way, we build an inclusive and strong society.

113155.explanation: Music lessons give children pleasure and talent. We believe every primary school child should have this opportunity. With subsidies from the municipality, we especially help families who otherwise cannot afford it. This ensures equal opportunities, creativity, and a strong foundation for the future.

113158.explanation: Safety starts with prevention. Therefore, we want more community officers focused mainly on prevention and cooperation with residents. Less emphasis on strict control, more on understanding and solutions. Together, we make Duiven safe, livable, and friendly for everyone.

113159.explanation: We are not against camera surveillance, but it must be done carefully. Only where necessary and proven effective. Privacy and residents' rights remain important. No uncontrolled growth, but targeted use. This way, we ensure safety and freedom together in Duiven.

113160.explanation: Safety is a basic right. We want a women's shelter in Duiven for those needing protection. With shelter and guidance, we give people a fresh start. Together, we ensure a municipality where respect and safety come first for everyone.

113165.explanation: We choose to build within village boundaries before looking at the countryside. This preserves nature and agriculture and makes smart use of space. With good planning, we provide housing and a green environment. Duiven remains livable and future-oriented.

113166.explanation: People on social assistance deserve opportunities. We encourage work, education, or volunteering, always with customized solutions and consultation. No coercion, no cuts. With a work bonus and cooperation, we make working attractive and bridge the gap to paid employment.

113168.explanation: We find it important that residents with a low income do not get into trouble. Remission helps participation and provides peace of mind. Together, we ensure a fair municipality where everyone has prospects and no one is left behind. This way, we create space for opportunities and the future.

113170.explanation: We are in favor, provided the municipality ensures a balanced distribution between regular parking spaces and charging spots, so that residents without electric cars are not disadvantaged and the accessibility of the neighborhood is maintained.

113171.explanation: Safe traffic is important. That is why we maintain a 30 km/h speed limit in built-up areas. It creates peace and prevents accidents. Together we ensure clear rules and safe streets where children can play and everyone can move freely. This makes our municipality livable.

113172.explanation: Everyone deserves good care, but not every preference can be guaranteed. The municipality facilitates where possible, without special claims based on conviction.

113173.explanation: Good youth care is about quality, not just price. Children deserve the best care. That is why we choose reliable organizations that collaborate and provide tailor-made solutions. Together we ensure that every child has the opportunity to grow up healthy and safe.

113174.explanation: A lower property tax means fewer resources for maintenance, safety, and services. We want a healthy budget and strong neighborhoods, not cuts that turn out to be more expensive later.

113176.explanation: Tourists contribute to our municipality. It is fair that they help pay for facilities. By increasing the tourist tax, we keep our environment clean and livable. Together we ensure quality and space for both residents and visitors.

113177.explanation: New industrial sites are only negotiable once existing locations are fully utilized. First, use what we already have smartly, then look at growth.

113181.explanation: We are against banning solar panels on agricultural land. It is better to make agreements that work for both agriculture and energy. This way we seize opportunities for sustainability and keep our municipality livable. Together we build a green and healthy future.

113182.explanation: No automatic extra funds and no rapid cuts. First, we want insight into usage, costs, and the future of De Ogtent. Based on facts, we decide what is needed and sensible for the entire municipality.

113183.explanation: School swimming is important, but structural financing by the municipality is not feasible. We opt for collaboration with parents, schools, and sports funds. This way, children learn to swim without unnecessary increases in municipal expenses.

113184.explanation: Dialect is valuable as heritage. We support initiatives to keep it visible. In education, the focus is on reading enjoyment, basic skills, and multilingualism. This ensures everyone learns to read and write well while making room for linguistic diversity.

113185.explanation: We consciously choose 30% because it is socially responsible without unnecessarily delaying new construction or making projects financially unfeasible. This results in enough affordable homes, but also space for other groups, which is essential for a healthy and diverse housing market in Duiven.

113187.explanation: By renting out individual rooms, we create space for people seeking housing. It is a practical solution that works for landlords and tenants. Together we ensure clear rules and a livable municipality where everyone can progress.

113188.explanation: We believe more parking spaces are needed, but in a smart way. New apartments get an indoor garage and parking on the roof is possible at shopping centers. This solves the shortage without taking up extra space.

113190.explanation: Looking ahead together gives us opportunities. Through reorganization, we strengthen our position and maintain quality in care and education. We protect the uniqueness of our communities and work towards a sustainable future. This way everyone feels involved and together we build a strong region.

113191.explanation: We want the meadows between Duiven and Groessen to remain open. The green landscape belongs to our villages and provides peace, space, and nature. By keeping this area undeveloped, the character of the environment is preserved.

113192.explanation: We want better traffic flow and accessibility. By smartly adjusting roads we ensure safety and less congestion. Together we make clear arrangements and build a municipality where everyone can move easily.

113135.explanation: The number of men and women does not have to be equal, but it can be. There can also be more women than men in a council. What matters is that they are a team and sufficiently competent.

113137.explanation: Small initiatives such as a public garden are fine. If the municipality cannot manage it itself, market parties, foundations, and/or associations can take on this task after agreements with the municipality.

113139.explanation: Every farmer must be able to do what he or she wants with their land up to a certain extent. For this, they can talk to the municipality.

113143.explanation: Where possible, it should be allowed, but this topic is currently not applicable in the municipality of Duiven.

113147.explanation: This is not a municipal task.

113150.explanation: The municipality must do everything together with the entrepreneur to make their building accessible to people with disabilities. Financial support can also be in the form of loans or subsidies.

113155.explanation: The municipality must provide sufficient subsidies to the music school so that it can offer music lessons at (primary) schools.

113158.explanation: The establishment of the National Police has resulted in the police being less visible in the municipality. Here there is an important task for the municipal enforcers.

113159.explanation: The municipality must commit itself to increasing the (social) safety of its residents.

113160.explanation: The shelter in a women's refuge is arranged by the Safety and Health Region Gelderland-Midden (VGGM) and the organization Veilig Thuis.

113165.explanation: In new construction, it is not about village boundaries but about municipal boundaries. You always build within the village and municipal boundaries.

113166.explanation: As far as possible, yes. To say must means it applies to everyone, but there are also situations where it is impossible to do socially meaningful work.

113168.explanation: The municipality must look at what a person has to spend and not so much at the income.

113170.explanation: At the moment there are sufficient charging points.

113171.explanation: This is better for traffic safety in the villages and also for the environment.

113172.explanation: When you need care, it should not matter who provides this care.

113173.explanation: The quality of youth care is the most important.

113174.explanation: The property tax should not be a source of easily obtained income for the municipality.

113176.explanation: The tourist tax in Duiven is already high, so a further increase is not what we want.

113177.explanation: The industrial sites also ensure sufficient employment.

113181.explanation: Agricultural land is for agriculture, not for solar panels.

113182.explanation: Social organizations must receive funding when discussions with these organizations show that their activities for society cannot or cannot fully be carried out.

113183.explanation: Financing school swimming is not a task of the municipality, but of the schools.

113184.explanation: The education program is not a task of the municipality.

113185.explanation: The percentage of social rental and affordable owner-occupied housing should be as high as possible, but especially houses must be built for the elderly, young people, starters and/or 1-2 person households.

113187.explanation: Room rental goes through the "Hospita rental" scheme. Allowing landlords to rent out individual rooms can lead to the problem that they rent out rooms as small as possible for very high amounts.

113188.explanation: The municipality of Duiven meets the parking standard.

113190.explanation: There is no use and no need to initiate a reorganization with another municipality. Duiven is strong enough to stand on its own. In many policy documents we follow one idea: "Relying on our own strength." We should also apply this to the reorganization discussion!

113191.explanation: This is also stated in the Environmental Vision. The meadows reduce heat stress and help in the retention of rainwater.

113192.explanation: The Plaksestraat must remain as it is. When developing the new residential area Plakse Weide, it is desirable that the Heiliglandsestraat is connected to the Oostsingel at a location to be determined.

statements.title: Duiven must remain an independent municipality.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Duiven needs more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Duiven.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to pay attention to Kleverlands (Liemers dialect).
A women's shelter (domestic violence refuge) should be established in Duiven.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The meadows between Duiven and Groessen must remain undeveloped.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
At least 40% of newly built homes in Duiven must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Duiven moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Business parks in Duiven may be allowed to expand.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Duiven should provide cultural center De Ogtent with more funding.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in Duiven.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The Plaksestraat between Heilighlandsestraat and Oostsingel should be widened and made accessible to through traffic.
